title: Debugging Techniques
subtitle: Find Bugs, Fix Problems!
overview: This session introduces students to debugging techniques used in programming. Students learn how to identify, understand, and fix errors in programs through fun activities and logical thinking exercises.

images._note: Add photos from the actual session — students finding mistakes in code, classroom activities, debugging games, whiteboard examples, teamwork photos.

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Students checking code for mistakes
gallery.caption: Debugging means finding and fixing errors

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Whiteboard example of syntax error
gallery.caption: Small mistakes can stop a program

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Students working together to solve bugs
gallery.caption: Teamwork helps in debugging

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Students playing debugging challenge game
gallery.caption: Find the hidden bug challenge!

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Students testing code in computer lab
gallery.caption: Testing programs step by step

keyTopics: What is Debugging? — Finding and fixing errors in a program
What is a Bug? — Mistakes in coding
Types of Errors — Syntax Error, Logical Error, Runtime Error
Using Print Statements to Check Output

youtubeVideos.desc: Simple explanation of debugging and coding mistakes for beginners.

youtubeVideos.title: Coding Mistakes and Solutions
youtubeVideos.desc: Understanding common coding mistakes and how to solve them.

activity.title: Find the Bug Challenge
steps: Teacher gives students a simple program with mistakes
Correct the errors step by step
Run the corrected program and test output

options: Finding and fixing errors in a program
quiz.explanation: Debugging means identifying and correcting mistakes in programs.

quiz.q: What is called a BUG in programming?
options: A mistake or error in code
quiz.explanation: A bug is an error or mistake in a program.

quiz.q: Which error happens because of wrong spelling or missing symbols?
quiz.explanation: Syntax errors happen when coding rules are broken.

quiz.q: Which error gives wrong output even when the program runs?
quiz.explanation: Logical errors make programs give incorrect results.

quiz.q: What should programmers do first when they see an error?
quiz.explanation: Error messages help programmers understand the problem.

options: An error while the program is running
quiz.explanation: Runtime errors occur during program execution.

quiz.q: Why do programmers test programs step by step?
quiz.explanation: Step-by-step testing helps locate errors quickly.

quiz.q: Which tool helps check values during debugging?
quiz.explanation: Print statements help programmers see outputs and values.
Debugging develops logical thinking and coding skills.

quiz.q: What should students do if a program does not work?
Why is patience important in debugging?
options: Because bugs can take time to fix
quiz.explanation: Finding errors sometimes requires careful thinking.

quiz.q: Which of these is NOT a type of programming error?
quiz.explanation: Lunch Error is not a programming error type.
Anyone who writes code may need debugging.

quiz.q: What did students learn from the Find the Bug Challenge?
options: Mistakes can be solved step by step
quiz.explanation: Debugging teaches problem-solving and careful thinking.
